episodes.title: Epstein & Trump: Where Are The 50 Pages?
episodes.description:
What’s really hidden inside the Epstein files—and what does it mean for Donald Trump?

In this episode, John Sweeney and Ruth Barnes explore missing pages linked to allegations involving Trump, and the wider web of intelligence connections surrounding Jeffrey Epstein. As questions grow over what’s been withheld, the story expands into a complex network of power, secrecy, and global influence.

With key details still hidden, one question remains…
what don’t they want the public to see?

Viewer discretion is advised.
This programme contains discussion of real crimes, including references to violence and distressing events, which some viewers may find upsetting. episodes.title: Epstein & Ghislaine: From High Society to Infamy?
episodes.description:
What shaped Ghislaine Maxwell long before Jeffrey Epstein?

Journalist John Sweeney and presenter Ruth Barnes explore the dark story behind the Maxwell family - and the powerful influence of media tycoon Robert Maxwell. From his traumatic childhood in Nazi-occupied Europe to the rise and collapse of his media empire at the Daily Mirror, they examine how power, trauma and corruption shaped one of the most infamous scandals of modern times.

This episode looks at Robert Maxwell’s mysterious death, the embezzlement of pension funds, Ghislaine’s entry into high society, and how she later became central to Epstein’s trafficking network.

Viewer discretion is advised.

episodes.title: Epstein & Mandelson: Exposing the British Elite?
episodes.description:
In this episode, John Sweeney and Ruth Barnes examine the alleged links between Prince Andrew and British politician Peter Mandelson in connection with Jeffrey Epstein. They discuss claims that Mandelson exchanged communications and potentially sensitive government information with Epstein, raising questions about corruption, political accountability, and the impact on public trust in British democracy. 

The conversation also explores Mandelson’s past controversies, the wider implications for the UK government, and how the scandal affects perceptions of both the monarchy and political elites.

Subscribe to It’s Criminal for more true crime investigations.

Viewer discretion is advised.

episodes.title: Andrew & Epstein: Follow the Money?
episodes.description:
The defining image of 2026: Prince Andrew leaning back in a car, avoiding the camera.

In Part 1 of The Epstein Files: The Consequences, John Sweeney and Ruth Barnes examine the Epstein files — and the political connections surrounding them, including Peter Mandelson. Arrested, interviewed and released under investigation — but not charged — what are the real legal risks?

They break down misconduct in public office, the £12 million settlement, the money trail, and why this case may hinge on finances rather than sex allegations.

The Prince and the Politician explores power, proximity, and accountability at the highest levels.

Prince Andrew denies wrongdoing and has not been charged.

Viewer discretion advised.
